zh.lifeSummary: 當Sarah Lees於1545年出生在英國諾廷加姆爾武德波洛時，她的父親Sir William Baker Lees21歲，母親Mary Thornleigh21歲。她於1564年與William Hollis結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。她去世於1600年8月在英國艾可莎佛朗 瓦爾登享年55歲，埋葬在英國艾可莎佛朗 瓦爾登。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sarah Lees于1545年出生在英国諾廷加姆爾武德波洛时，她的父亲Sir William Baker Lees21岁，母亲Mary Thornleigh21岁。她于1564年与William Hollis结婚。他们至少是2个女儿的父母。她去世于1600年8月在英国艾可莎佛朗 瓦爾登享年55岁，埋葬在英国艾可莎佛朗 瓦爾登。

nameMeanings.name: Lees
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: topographic name from Middle English lees ‘fields, arable land’, plural of lee (see Lee ), or from Middle English lese ‘pasture, meadow’ (Old English lǣs).

English: habitational name fromLeece or Lees in Lancashire, or Leese in Cheshire, all named from OldEnglish lēas ‘woodland clearings’ (plural oflēah), or from Leece in Cumbria, which was probably namedwith a Celtic word, lïss ‘hall, court, the principalhouse in a district’.

variants.name: Leece
meanings: English: from a medieval female personal name, Lece, a short form of Lettice (from Latin Laetitia, meaning ‘happiness, gaiety’).
Irish: shortened form of Gaelic Mac Giolla Íosa, Manx Mac Guilley Yeesey ‘son of Giolla Íosa (devotee or servant of Jesus)’. Ballaleece (‘Leece's farm’) in Kirk German was the family's main seat.

variants.name: Lee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sang, Jae, Jong, Jung, Sung, Yong, Kyung, Seung, Dong, Kwang, Myung.

English: topographic name for someone who lived near a meadow or a patch of arable land, Middle English lee, lea, from Old English lēa, dative case (used after a preposition) of lēah, which originally meant ‘wood or glade’.

English: habitational name from any of the many places in England named with Old English lēah ‘wood, glade’, including Lee in Buckinghamshire, Essex, Hampshire, Kent, and Shropshire, and Lea in Cheshire, Derbyshire, Herefordshire, Lancashire, Lincolnshire, and Wiltshire.

nameMeanings.name: Sarah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the wife of Abraham and mother of Isaac. According to the Book of Genesis, she was originally called Sarai (possibly meaning ‘contentious’ in Hebrew), but had her name changed by God to the more auspicious Sarah ‘princess’ in token of a greater blessing (Genesis 17:15, ‘And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall her name be’). This has been one of the most enduringly popular girls' names.

variants.name: Sara
meanings: Variant of   Sarah .  This is the form used in the Greek of the New Testament (Hebrews 11:11).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Sarah Lees was born in 1545, in Woodborough, Nottinghamshire, England, her father, Sir William Baker Lees, was 21 and her mother, Mary Thornleigh, was 21. She married William Hollis in 1564. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. She died in August 1600, in Saffron Walden, Essex, England, at the age of 55, and was buried in Saffron Walden, Essex, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Sarah Lees(이)가 1545년 영국 노틴그함실르 우드버루그h에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir William Baker Lees(은)는 21세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mary Thornleigh(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1564년 William Hollis(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1600년 8월 영국 에세스 사프런 월든에서 55세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 에세스 사프런 월든에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sarah Lees родилась в 1545 г. в местности вудбороую, ноттингхамшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir William Baker Lees было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Mary Thornleigh, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Hollis в 1564 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Она умерла август 1600 г. в местности саффрон валден, эссекс, Англия в возрасте 55 лет и была похоронена в местности саффрон валден, эссекс, Англия.
